evidence: Module header 'Twin prime conjecture' cites the Twin prime conjecture Wikipedia sections; the decl states 'Are there infinitely many primes p such that p + 2 is prime?'
verify_note: decl exists @research open; statement 'infinitely many p with p+2 prime' IS the twin prime conjecture; QID live, label matches (no enwiki sitelink but item is the conjecture)

evidence: The statement quantifies over primes p with p + 2 prime, i.e. twin primes.
verify_note: decl exists; twin primes are the quantified objects; QID live, label matches

evidence: Module header 'Union-closed sets conjecture' with Wikipedia reference; the decl states the conjecture: some element belongs to at least half the sets of any union-closed family other than {empty}.
verify_note: decl exists @research open; states the union-closed sets conjecture exactly; QID live, label matches

evidence: Docstring: Yu [Yu23] showed the union-closed sets conjecture holds with constant ~0.38234 instead of 1/2.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; Yu's 0.38234 variant of the conjecture; QID live

evidence: Docstring: Vuckovic and Zivkovic proved the union-closed sets conjecture for universal sets of cardinality at most 12.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; conjecture restricted to |universe|<=12; QID live

evidence: Docstring: Roberts and Simpson showed the union-closed sets conjecture holds for families of size at most 50.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; conjecture for families of size <=50; QID live

evidence: Docstring: the union-closed sets conjecture is true for universal set of cardinality 2, by brute force.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; conjecture for Fin 2 universe; QID live

evidence: Docstring: the union-closed sets conjecture is true when the family contains a singleton.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; conjecture when a singleton is in the family; QID live

evidence: Docstring: the union-closed sets conjecture is sharp — replacing 1/2 by any larger constant makes it fail.
verify_note: decl exists @research solved; sharpness of the 1/2 constant in the conjecture; QID live

evidence: Docstring: if the UC conjecture is tight for a family A then |A| = 2^k (Conjecture 3 in the cited Nieuw Archief note).
verify_note: decl exists @research open; tightness variant of the conjecture; QID live

evidence: Module header 'Vaught conjecture' cites the Wikipedia article; the decl states the number of countable models of a complete countable theory is at most aleph_0 or exactly continuum.
verify_note: decl exists @research open; states Vaught's conjecture on countable models; QID live, label matches
